_note: MEASURED on the follower (/dev/ttyACM1) on 2026-07-12 through gentle_move's load-watch, after the measurement fix. Each bound is either a wall the arm actually met (contact detected DURING the move) or the encoder limit. POSE-DEPENDENT: joints were probed one at a time, so each range holds for the pose the previous probes left the arm in. The shoulders cannot descend because the arm rests on its work surface. This is not a substitute for the joint-COMBINATION map that `arm explore` is meant to build.
_caveat_elbow_flex: elbow_flex's encoder WRAPS within its physical travel: driven toward 4095 it rotated past the 4095->0 boundary and read back as ~1. Its encoder value is therefore NOT monotonic with its angle, a [min,max] pair cannot represent its reachable set, and gentle_move cannot converge on a goal across the wrap (the arrival check compares linear ticks, so such a move exits on timeout instead). The range recorded below is the CONTIGUOUS, WRAP-FREE region: above its measured wall at 2020, stopping short of the wrap. Fixing this properly needs an encoder re-zero (calibration), not a map entry.
_caveat_wrist_roll: wrist_roll has the SAME wrap problem, and its 'free all the way round' result is exactly why: a joint that rotates through its whole range necessarily crosses the 4095->0 boundary, so its recorded [21, 4073] is not a pair of walls -- it is the encoder seam, and the joint passes straight through it. CONFIRMED 2026-07-12: parked at position 4 (i.e. ON the seam) and commanded to 304, the joint could not converge and the move correctly ran to its 7.0s timeout, honestly reporting the position it actually reached (3055) rather than claiming arrival. Treat wrist_roll as UNBOUNDED/continuous, and do not plan a move for it across the seam until the wrap is modelled (same underlying fix as elbow_flex: an encoder re-zero, or wrap-aware distance in gentle_move).

_walls.wrist_roll: none - free through its whole range
